evals.prompt: Should I bundle? My AOV is stuck at $48.
evals.expected_output: Walks through the bundle-vs-AOV diagnostic order before recommending bundles. Should check natural SKU affinity first, then existing bundle merchandising, then absence of bundles, then discount discipline. Should not jump to 'yes, build a bundle' without diagnosis.
assertions: Walks through the four-step bundle-vs-AOV diagnostic order
Calls modern.sales.sku_affinity to check natural affinity
Calls modern.sales.aov to check AOV trend, not just level
Does not jump to bundle recommendation without affinity check
Asks about catalog and discount frequency
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: I want to make a starter bundle for first-time customers. What should be in it?
evals.expected_output: Applies the affinity-based composition framework. Recommends a two-SKU bundle (three-SKU costs trust at first purchase), anchored to a top-seller, with complementary not substitutable items, with at least 5% co-purchase affinity in unbundled orders. Should pull affinity data and recommend specific SKU pairs.
assertions: Recommends two-SKU bundle for first-time customers (not three-SKU)
Recommends anchor to top-seller SKU
Names the complementary-not-substitutable rule
Names the 5% affinity threshold
Calls modern.sales.sku_affinity to identify candidates
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: Is it better to discount a single product or to bundle it with something else?
evals.expected_output: Walks through the bundle-vs-single-SKU discount strategy. Bundle discount preserves the single-SKU price floor; single-SKU discount erodes it. Should describe the cannibalization check and explain that for AOV-driving brands, bundle discount is the safer long-term lever.
assertions: Recommends bundle discount over single-SKU discount for price-floor preservation
Explains why single-SKU discount erodes pricing power
Describes the cannibalization check using affinity data
Notes when single-SKU discount may still be appropriate (acquisition)
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: I'm thinking about a 'good better best' structure for my bundles. How big should each tier be?
evals.expected_output: Walks through anchor pricing and good-better-best. Tier 1: 2-SKU, 15% off, broad appeal. Tier 2: 3-4 SKU, 20-25% off, intended winner. Tier 3: 5+ SKU, 30-35% off, anchor. Should explain why the anchor tier is critical even though it sells low volume.
assertions: Describes three tiers with sizes and discount percentages
Names Tier 2 as the intended winner
Explains why Tier 3 anchor sells low volume but does perceptual work
Cites the 20-40% Tier 2 sales drop when anchor is removed
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: I'm a supplement brand. Should I bundle sleep aids together or mix categories?
evals.expected_output: Applies the bundle composition rules. Three sleep aids in one bundle creates a choice problem; the customer doesn't know which one to take when. Better to bundle for a use case (e.g. Sleep Better Pack with sleep aid + magnesium for muscle relaxation + tea), creating a sequential routine. Should name the cross-category vs within-category outperformance.
assertions: Recommends use-case bundling over category bundling
Names the cross-category vs within-category outperformance
Names the sequential-use vs parallel-use rule
Calls modern.sales.sku_affinity to identify cross-category pairs
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: Where should I show my bundles? On the product page, in the cart, or after purchase?
evals.expected_output: Walks through sequential vs simultaneous reveal. Simultaneous (product page) sets AOV ceiling. Sequential (cart upgrade) captures committed customers. Post-purchase seeds the next-order conversation. Recommends mixing placements rather than relying on one.
assertions: Describes all three reveal placements
Recommends mixing placements rather than relying on one
Names which placement raises AOV most at the decision moment
Names which placement has highest attach rate per impression
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: What should I name my bundles?
evals.expected_output: Applies the bundle naming framework. Outcome names beat content names. 'The Morning Routine' beats 'Cleanser + Toner + Moisturizer Bundle.' The name should answer 'why buy?' not 'what is this?' Should give examples specific to the user's category.
assertions: Recommends outcome-based naming over content-based naming
Gives 'Morning Routine' vs 'Cleanser+Toner+Moisturizer' style examples
Explains the name answers 'why buy?' not 'what is this?'
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections
